name: Polar Stratospheric Cloud
primary_sources: Polar Stratospheric Clouds Above Spitsbergen at Alfred Wegener Institute

distinctive_features: Iridescence due to diffraction of sunlight
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Extends over large areas at altitudes of 15,000 to 25,000 meters

functional_attributes.primary_function: Reflect sunlight and contribute to ozone depletion

domain_specific_functional.OZONE_DEPLETION_ROLE: Supports chemical reactions that produce active chlorine
domain_specific_functional.LIGHT_SCATTERING: Reflects sunlight from below the horizon

relationships.description: Type I PSCs support chemical reactions that lead to ozone depletion
Commonly observed in polar stratosphere during winter

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents extreme atmospheric conditions and environmental change
primary_sources: Polar Stratospheric Clouds Above Spitsbergen at Alfred Wegener Institute
